questions.question: If matrix A = [[2, -1], [3, 4]] and matrix B = [[1, 0], [-2, 5]], find the determinant of AB.
Matrix M = [[a, b], [c, d]] has determinant 6. If every entry is multiplied by –2, what is the determinant of the resulting matrix?
questions.explanation: Scaling all entries of a 2×2 matrix by factor k multiplies the determinant by k². Here k = –2, so the new determinant = 6 × (–2)² = 6 × 4 = 24.

questions.question: Given A = [[1, 2, 0], [0, 3, 1], [2, –1, 4]], calculate the determinant using cofactor expansion along the first row.
Two matrices P and Q satisfy PQ = I (the identity matrix). If det(P) = 1/5, what is det(Q)?
questions.explanation: Since PQ = I, det(PQ) = det(P) × det(Q) = det(I) = 1. Therefore, (1/5) × det(Q) = 1, so det(Q) = 5.

questions.question: Matrix X = [[2, 3], [1, k]] has a determinant of 0. What is the value of k?
questions.explanation: For a singular matrix, det(X) = 2k – 3(1) = 0. Solving: 2k – 3 = 0, so k = 3/2.

questions.question: If C = [[4, –2], [6, 3]] and you need to find C², determine the (1,2) entry of C².
questions.explanation: The (1,2) entry of C² equals row 1 of C times column 2 of C: (4)(–2) + (–2)(3) = –8 – 6 = –14.
